subject: DevOps
title: SRE and Developer Collaboration
description: A fast breakdown of SRE and Developer Collaboration for builders.

hook.text: Devs ship fast. SREs want stability. The tension can be toxic or become productive balance.

buildup.text: SRE and dev teams need shared goals. Error budgets provide exactly that.

discovery.text: Embed SREs in sprints. They review designs for reliability and suggest monitoring early.

twist.text: Dev teams should share on-call. Feeling production pain improves code quality naturally.

climax.text: Shared ownership removes the blame game. Both teams win when the service is reliable.

punchline.text: Partners, not opponents. Shared goals build collaboration.

quiz.question: How can SRE and development teams collaborate effectively?
options: By sharing goals through error budgets and embedding SREs in sprints
By keeping the teams completely separate
By having developers handle all on-call duties alone
